entry.title: SmartRecruiters Implementation & Product questions April 2026
entry.content:
Below are several SR questions with answers

 

Q1: Controlling Fields Visible to the Job Creator ("Originator")

SmartRecruiters does not have a dedicated "Originator" role in the traditional sense. However, you can control what the job creator sees through these mechanisms:

User Role at Creation Time

  • The person creating the job is governed by their assigned User Role (e.g., Recruiter, Hiring Manager, HR Admin). Field visibility and editability are tied to that role's configuration.
  • Go to Admin → User Roles → [Role] → Job Fields Permissions to configure which fields are visible, required, or editable per role.

"Create Job" Screen Configuration

  • SmartRecruiters allows you to configure the job creation wizard (the initial form) separately from the full job req form. You can limit which fields appear during the creation step via Admin → Company Settings → Jobs → Job Fields.
  • Fields can be set to: Hidden, Visible (read-only), or Editable per role.

Practical Workaround

  • Assign the job creator a specific role (e.g., "Job Creator" or "Recruiter") before they create the job. Since roles are user-level, not job-level at creation time, the user's default role governs what they see.
  • If you want stricter control, use Approval Workflows to lock fields after creation so post-creation editing is role-gated.

Key limitation: There is no dynamic "originator" role that auto-assigns based on who clicked "Create Job." The workaround is ensuring users have the correct default role pre-assigned.

Q2: Removing Standard Fields Like "Salary Range" from the Job Req

Yes, with limitations:

  • Custom job fields you create can be fully hidden or removed per role.
  • Standard/system fields (like Salary Range in the Position Information section) can often be hidden at the role level via Job Field Permissions, but cannot be permanently deleted from the system schema.
  • Navigate to: Admin → User Roles → [Role] → Job Fields and set Salary Range to "Hidden" for roles that should not see it.
  • For fields that are locked by SAP and cannot be hidden via UI, you would need to raise a support request with SmartRecruiters or use configuration consulting to suppress them.

Note: Some fields tied to integrations (e.g., HRIS sync) may be forcibly visible. Always test in a sandbox first.

 Q3: Preventing Candidates from Editing Application Answers After Submission

Out-of-the-box behavior: SmartRecruiters allows candidates to edit their application via the Candidate Portal until a certain stage in some configurations, but there is no native toggle to hard-lock answers immediately after submission.

Options available:

  • Application Form Design: Set application questions as non-editable by design — but this is limited natively.
  • Candidate Portal Settings: Under Admin → Career Site → Candidate Experience, review whether "Edit Application" is surfaced.
  • Workaround via Workflow Stage: Move candidates automatically to a stage (e.g., "Under Review") immediately upon submission using automation rules — some stage progressions restrict candidate-side edits.
  • Recommended: Raise this as a product enhancement request.

 

Q4: Candidate Profile & Logging Back In Without Applying

SmartRecruiters does have a Candidate Profile concept, though it works differently from some ATS platforms:

  • Candidates create a profile when they first apply via the Career Portal.
  • They can log back in at [yourcompany].smartrecruiters.com/candidate using the credentials created during application.
  • From their profile, they can: view application status, update profile details, and apply to new jobs — without needing to re-apply to access their account.
  • The profile stores resume, personal info, and application history.

To log in without applying:

  • Direct candidates to your Career Site → "Sign In" link. They can access their existing profile without initiating a new application.

If candidates report not being able to find the login, ensure the "Sign In" option is enabled and visible on your Career Site configuration.

 

Q5: Adding Job Field Tokens to Job Postings / Advertisements

This is a known limitation in SmartRecruiters' Career Page builder:

Standard Behavior:

  • The Job Ad / Posting template supports a limited set of merge tokens (job title, department, location, etc.).
  • The Career Page drag-and-drop editor natively exposes only ~2 job-level tokens for dynamic content.

Workarounds to bring in custom job fields as tokens:

Approach

Details

Job Description Body

Embed custom field values directly in the job description text manually or via an integration

API Integration

Use the SmartRecruiters API to pull job fields and inject them into a custom-built career page outside the native builder

Custom Career Site (CSS/JS)

If you use a custom-hosted career site, you can call the SR Open API to fetch and render any job field dynamically

SmartRecruiters Professional Services

Request custom token support — enterprise agreements sometimes allow extended token sets

Best practice: For rich, field-driven postings, consider building a custom career site using SR's Open API rather than relying on the native page builder.

 

Q6: Best Practices for Creating a Non-Position-Managed Evergreen Req

An Evergreen Req is used for continuous/high-volume hiring where you pool candidates over time.

Setup Best Practices:

  1. Create as a "Pipeline" or "Pooling" Job
    • Do not tie it to a specific headcount or position in your org chart.
    • Set Number of Openings to a high number (e.g., 99) or use "Unlimited" if available.
  2. Naming Convention
    • Use a clear naming pattern: "[Department] – Evergreen Pool – [Year]" to distinguish from active reqs.
  3. Job Status Management
    • Keep the job in "Always Open" status; use Automation Rules to move candidates from the evergreen req to specific active reqs when a real opening arises.
  4. Sourcing
    • Post to job boards with evergreen-appropriate messaging ("Join our talent community").
    • Use Talent Pool tagging to segment candidates within the req.
  5. Avoid Offer/Hire Actions Directly on the Evergreen Req
    • Use it purely as a sourcing and screening pipeline; transfer finalists to a positional req for offer and hire steps to keep analytics clean.
  6. Review Cadence
    • Set a periodic review (monthly/quarterly) to disposition stale candidates to maintain pool quality.

 

Q7: What Drives Hiring Cost Calculations in the Analytics Dashboard?

SmartRecruiters' Cost of Hire analytics pulls from:

Cost Driver

Source

Job Board Posting Costs

Integrated via SmartDistribute (vonq) or manually entered job board budgets

Agency/Vendor Fees

Entered via the Offer or Vendor Management module

Internal Recruiter Cost

Configured as a daily/hourly rate per recruiter in Admin settings

Time-to-Fill × Recruiter Rate

Calculated automatically based on req open date and recruiter cost rate

Referral Bonuses

If tracked within SR

To ensure accurate data:

  • Set up Recruiter Cost Rates under Admin → Analytics Settings.
  • Ensure job board spend is fed back via SmartDistribute integration (vonq)
  • Configure your cost center mappings if using HRIS integration.

 

Q8: Posting a Job to Multiple Locations in the Same Country

Since Location is a single-select field, options include:

  • Duplicate the Req — Create one req per location; link them via a common requisition family or job family code for reporting.
  • Use a Generic Location — Set location to the country or region level (e.g., "Germany – Multiple Locations") and specify details in the job description body.
  • Multi-Location Field (if enabled) — Some SR configurations support a secondary "Additional Locations" field; check with your SR admin if this is active on your instance.
  • SmartRecruiters Multi-Location Feature — Enterprise plans may have access to a multi-location posting feature — confirm with your CSM.

 

Q9: Posting a Job in Multiple Languages

SmartRecruiters handles multilingual postings as follows:

Native Approach:

  • Each language version of a job requires a separate job req or a separate job ad within the same req.
  • Under the job, go to Job Ad → Add Language Version — this allows you to create translated versions of the posting text within a single req.

Steps:

  1. Open the Job → Job Ad tab
  2. Click "Add Language"
  3. Select the target language and enter the translated content
  4. Publish — candidates will see the version matching their browser/portal language setting

Note: Job fields (title, department, etc.) remain in the base language. Only the Job Ad body (description, requirements) supports multi-language versions natively.

 

Q10: Blocking Candidates from Changing Interview Timeslots After Self-Scheduling

Native SmartRecruiters behavior:

  • Once a candidate self-schedules via SmartRecruiters Interview Scheduling, they can typically reschedule until the interview time if the link/portal remains active.

Options to restrict rescheduling:

  • Expire the Scheduling Link — After the candidate books, manually expire or deactivate the scheduling link so it can no longer be used to change the slot.
  • Automation Rule — Trigger an automation once a slot is booked to move the candidate to a stage where the scheduling action is no longer available.
  • Communicate via Confirmation Email — Use the confirmation email template to set the expectation that rescheduling requires contacting the recruiter directly (soft control).
  • Third-Party Scheduling Integration — If using GoodTime, Calendly, or Prelude via integration, those tools offer hard locks on rescheduling after a configurable window.

Hard technical lock on self-scheduling changes is not natively available in SR out of the box. The most robust solution is using a dedicated scheduling integration that supports rescheduling restrictions.

entry.title: job level - pay range association
entry.content:
Hi ,

We want the pay range to be determined solely based on job level, which is maintained in a custom MDF object within Job Classification, rather than the pay grade maintained in Job Information. 

Specifically, the pay range should be derived from a custom MDF field (ABC) in Job Information, then Job Level(12) in the Job Classification object. We do not want the Pay Grade (SG1) foundation object to be part of this association. However, all employees will continue to have a pay grade assigned in Job Information, and we do not intend to change that.

Given this scenario, please advise on the best approach to create the pay range association in the Corporate Data Model. We also want to use these pay ranges in Compensation Planning, 

 

Thank you

entry.title: 🚀 CodeJam Curitiba: quando a comunidade mostra a sua força
entry.content:
Tem eventos que a gente organiza...E tem eventos que a gente vive...O CodeJam Curitiba foi vivido.

Desde cedo já dava para sentir que seria especial. A recepção com coffee, conversas animadas e aquela energia boa de quem estava ali para aprender de verdade. E, claro, a presença das lendas Thomas, Rich e Josh já elevava o nível da expectativa.

 

 

 

 

Às 9h iniciamos oficialmente. Nosso curador  @LuizSouza abriu o evento reforçando algo que eu acredito profundamente: comunidade não é sobre evento, é sobre conexão. É sobre troca real.

 

 

 

 

Na sequência, o professor Valter, representando a Escola Politécnica PUCPR, falou sobre a importância dessa aproximação entre universidade e mercado. E que privilégio poder realizar esse momento dentro de uma instituição que abriu as portas com tanto carinho.

 

 

 

 

Nosso patrocinador CHPRO, por meio do Marcelo Passaglia, trouxe algo que eu particularmente valorizo muito: trajetória. Histórias inspiram. Pessoas reais inspiram. E ver esse apoio genuíno ao ecossistema mostra que estamos no caminho certo.

 

 

 

 

E então, Josh. Energia pura. Daquelas que contagiam, que lembram por que a SAP Community é diferente.

 

 

 

 

 

🤖Mão na massa — e é aqui que a mágica acontece

Depois das falas, fomos para o hands-on. E que experiência.

Thomas e Rich trouxeram muitas novidades sobre IA e mostraram, na prática, o tamanho das possibilidades que temos nas mãos. Mas o que mais me marcou não foi só o conteúdo técnico.

 

 

 

Foi a proximidade.

Ver Thomas e Rich lado a lado com os participantes, sentados juntos, trocando, ensinando, aprendendo… isso é comunidade de verdade. Não é palco distante. É construção conjunta.

Na parte da tarde, o ambiente era de concentração, curiosidade e descoberta. Era bonito de ver.

🎁Encerramento com aquele sentimento de “quero mais”

Finalizamos com o tradicional sorteio, a foto oficial e mais um coffee para aquele networking que gera futuras conexões e oportunidades.

 

 

 

 

 

E sim… nossa mascote capivara marcou presença e roubou sorrisos por onde passou 🧡

Encerramos o dia com aquele sentimento coletivo de que algo importante começou.

💙Gratidão (daquelas sinceras)

Eu acredito que eventos assim só acontecem porque existem pessoas que acreditam junto.

Nossa gratidão:

À @antonia_zorn , da SAP Community, por confiar, escolher Curitiba e nos incluir na rota.

Ao Thomas e Rich, por compartilharem conhecimento com tanta generosidade.

Ao Josh, pela energia e por fortalecer continuamente essa comunidade global.

Ao nosso patrocinador CHPRO, que apoiou de coração aberto e tornou tudo possível.

À Escola Politécnica PUCPR, por nos receber como casa.

À Vitória e ao Vitor, que vieram da SAP São Leopoldo para apoiar os instrutores e fortalecer ainda mais a experiência do CodeJam.

Aos nossos curadores @SimoneAzevedo , @LuizSouza e @AdrianoCWB , que planejaram e executaram cada detalhe com dedicação.

 

 

 

 

 

E um agradecimento muito especial aos participantes.

Vocês se inscreveram, confiaram, compareceram e provaram algo muito importante:

Curitiba tem sede de conhecimento. Curitiba tem protagonismo. Curitiba tem força.

Vocês mostraram, na prática, o poder da nossa comunidade quando se une em torno de tecnologia, aprendizado e colaboração.

 

🎬Veja os melhores momentos

Os registros estão no vídeo Melhores momentos— mas a experiência, essa fica na memória.

Curitiba entrou no mapa do CodeJam.

E eu tenho certeza: esse é só o começo.

Que venham muitos outros.

Que venham mais conexões.

Que venham mais histórias para contar.

Nos vemos nos próximos eventos da SAP Community Curitiba.

Com carinho,

Simone Azevedo 💙

entry.title: RAG For Document Analysis
entry.content:
Introduction

Large Language Models (LLMs) have transformed how we interact with data, enabling natural language understanding and generation at unprecedented levels. Yet, despite their capabilities, they are inherently limited by the knowledge embedded during training and often referred to as parametric memory. This limitation becomes evident when models are tasked with answering questions that require up-to-date, proprietary, or domain-specific information.

Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) emerges as a powerful paradigm to address this gap. By integrating external knowledge sources into the generation process, RAG enables LLMs to produce responses that are not only coherent but also factually grounded and contextually relevant.

Understanding the RAG Paradigm

At its core, RAG separates knowledge storage from reasoning capability.

  • Parametric Knowledge: Encoded within the model during training
  • Non-Parametric Knowledge: Stored externally in a searchable repository

This separation allows systems to remain flexible and continuously updated without the need for expensive retraining.

A useful analogy is the distinction between a closed-book exam and an open-book exam. Traditional LLMs operate like the former, relying solely on what they have learned. RAG-enabled systems, however, can reference external materials, enabling more accurate and informed responses.

The RAG Workflow

 

 

A typical RAG pipeline consists of these stages: 

  • Document Ingestion and Chunking

The process begins with ingesting documents from various sources such as PDFs, Word files, or structured data formats. These documents are divided into smaller, manageable segments or chunks. Effective chunking is critical, as it directly impacts the quality of retrieval.

  • Embedding Generation

Each chunk is transformed into a vector representation using an embedding model. These embeddings capture the semantic meaning of the text, allowing similar pieces of information to be located efficiently.

  • Vector Storage

The embeddings are stored in a vector database, such as Chroma, FAISS, or Pinecone. This database functions as an external memory layer, enabling rapid similarity searches.

  • Retrieval

When a user submits a query, it is converted into an embedding and compared against the stored vectors. The system retrieves the top-k most relevant chunks, forming the contextual foundation for the response.

  • Augmentation

The retrieved content is combined with the user query using a structured prompt template. This step ensures the language model has access to the most relevant information before generating a response.

  • Generation

Finally, the augmented prompt is passed to the LLM, which synthesizes a response grounded in both its internal knowledge and the retrieved context.

Why RAG Over Fine-Tuning?

Historically, adapting models to domain-specific tasks required fine-tuning, a process that is resource-intensive and inflexible. RAG offers a more efficient alternative:

  • Dynamic Knowledge Updates: No need to retrain the model when data changes
  • Cost Efficiency: Eliminates repeated training cycles
  • Scalability: Easily adapts to growing datasets
  • Modularity: Components can be independently optimized

This makes RAG particularly well-suited for environments where information evolves rapidly.

Benefits of RAG

Organizations adopting RAG can expect several advantages:

  • Improved Accuracy: Responses are grounded in real, retrievable data
  • Reduced Hallucinations: Minimizes unsupported or fabricated outputs
  • Real-Time Relevance: Easily incorporates the latest information
  • Transparency: Enables traceability to source documents
  • Domain Adaptability: Works seamlessly with specialized datasets

Challenges and Considerations

Despite its advantages, implementing RAG effectively requires careful design:

  • Chunking Strategy: Poor segmentation can degrade retrieval quality
  • Embedding Selection: Model choice significantly impacts performance
  • Latency: Retrieval adds overhead to response time
  • Prompt Engineering: Poorly structured prompts can limit effectiveness
  • Evaluation Metrics: Measuring success requires more than traditional accuracy metrics

Addressing these challenges is essential for building robust, production-ready systems.

Conclusion

Retrieval-Augmented Generation represents a significant evolution in the design of intelligent systems. By decoupling knowledge from reasoning, it enables LLMs to operate with greater accuracy, flexibility, and relevance.

For document analysis, RAG is not just an enhancement, it is a foundational capability that transforms how organizations interact with information. As data continues to grow in volume and complexity, RAG will play a central role in enabling systems that are not only intelligent, but also reliable and context-aware.

entry.title: Reforma Tributária Brasil 2026: Impactos no SAP e o "Plano de Voo" para a Transição (Convite)
entry.content:
Olá, SAP Community!

Já estamos em 2026 e a "fase de teoria" da Reforma Tributária brasileira acabou. Para nós, profissionais que trabalhamos com o ecossistema SAP, este é o momento em que a legislação sai do papel e precisa rodar dentro do sistema.

O grande desafio técnico e de negócios deste ano não é apenas a nova alíquota, mas sim a convivência dos sistemas tributários. Como manter o seu ERP (seja ECC ou S/4HANA) operando com o modelo atual enquanto inicia os testes e validações do IVA Dual (IBS + CBS)?

A complexidade de localização no Brasil sempre foi alta, e agora, com a necessidade de um "motor de cálculo" híbrido durante a transição, o risco de paradas operacionais ou inconsistências fiscais aumenta significativamente.

O Plano de Voo para 2026

Para discutir exatamente como superar esses desafios técnicos e estratégicos, a CVA, parceira especializada em consultoria SAP, uniu forças com a Avalara, líder em automação fiscal.

Na próxima semana, dia 21 de Janeiro (quarta-feira), realizaremos um webinar focado em casos práticos e arquitetura de solução.

Não vamos falar (apenas) de leis. Vamos falar de como a tecnologia suporta a lei.

O que vamos abordar:

  • Cenário Q1 2026: O que priorizar na configuração do sistema agora no início do ano.

  • Desafio da Convivência: Como gerenciar a apuração antiga e a nova simultaneamente sem customizações excessivas (Z) que engessam o core digital.

  • Automação via Tax Engine: Como a integração nativa da Avalara resolve a complexidade de cálculo, tirando o peso do ERP.

  • Cases: Lições aprendidas de projetos que já iniciaram a adaptação.

Os Palestrantes:

Teremos uma discussão técnica e executiva com:

  • Juliano Kisielewicz (Chief Revenue Officer na CVA) – Trazendo a visão de processos e estratégia SAP.

  • Luiz Fernando Albertin (Sales Engineer Head na Avalara) – Trazendo a visão técnica da solução fiscal.

Como Participar:

O evento é online e gratuito, aberto a consultores, arquitetos de solução, key-users e gestores fiscais que utilizam SAP.

📅 Data: 21 de Janeiro de 2026
🕙 Horário: 10h00
🔗Link para inscriçao

Se você está trabalhando na estratégia de localização da sua empresa para este ano, este é um conteúdo essencial para o seu planejamento.

Nos vemos lá!

entry.title: AP Aging Query Not Matching SAP Business One Aging Report
entry.content:
Hi Experts,
I am currently developing a custom query for the Accounts Payable Aging report in SAP Business One. However, the total amount returned by my query does not match the standard SAP Business One Aging report.
Below is the query I am using:

SELECT T0.[DocNum], T0.[CardCode], T0.[CardName], T0.[DocDate], T0.[DocTotal] FROM OPCH T0 INNER JOIN OVPM T1 ON T0.[ReceiptNum] = T1.[DocEntry] INNER JOIN OACT T2 ON T0.[CtlAccount] = T2.[AcctCode] WHERE
T0.[CANCELED] ='N' and T0.[DocDate] <=[%0]
and
(T0.[DocStatus]='O' or T0.[DocStatus]<>'O' and T1.[DocDate] >[%0])
and
(
T2.[AcctCode]='403100' or
T2.[AcctCode]='403200' or
T2.[AcctCode]='403300' or
T2.[AcctCode]='403400' or
T2.[AcctCode]='403500'
) and T1.[Canceled] <>'Y'

My requirement is to include:
Open invoices as of the selected date
Closed invoices that were paid after the selected date
Anyone can help me please

entry.title: SAP DM Inventory Transfer Valuation TYPE
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

I am facing an issue while performing a 311 movement for a non-batch-managed PRT material that is subject to split valuation.

During the MIGO transfer posting in S/4HANA, the system prompts for a valuation type in order to post the document. However, when the corresponding IDoc is generated for inventory download, the system incorrectly populates the batch number field with the valuation type.

This results in two issues:

  1. Goods movement failure (311 transfer posting):
    The system attempts to fetch a batch based on the batch number, which in this case is actually the valuation type. Since the material is not batch-managed, the posting fails.
  2. Goods issue restriction in consumption plugin:
    When attempting goods issuance, the system requires a valuation type to be entered. However, the material consumption plugin does not provide a field to input or select a valuation type during consumption, making it impossible to proceed with the goods issue.

Has anyone encountered a similar behavior in S/4HANA or SAP DM integration scenarios involving split valuation materials and non-batch-managed PRTs? Any guidance or recommended configuration would be greatly appreciated.

Best regards,
Aziz Khan

SAP Digital Manufacturing SAP Digital Manufacturing Insights

entry.title: Subject: Clarification on Address Data Passed in Vertex Integration for SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi all,

We are currently evaluating the standard integration between SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud and Vertex for tax determination and had a question around how address data is handled in the delivered integration.

From the SAP-delivered Vertex integration flows (Calculate Tax, Post Tax, and Determine Tax Jurisdiction Code), we understand that the integration is standardized and relies on predefined APIs/iFlows. However, it is not entirely clear from the available documentation what level of address detail is included in the request sent to Vertex.

Architecture context:
Our current landscape includes:

  • Front-end order entry systems (OES / eCommerce platforms)

  • SAP BTP Integration Suite as the middleware layer

  • SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud as the system of record

  • Vertex as the external tax engine

We are evaluating two possible patterns:

  1. SAP-driven tax calculation:
    Front-end → SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud → Vertex

  2. Upstream tax calculation via integration layer:
    Front-end → Integration Suite → Vertex → SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud

Questions:

  • What address elements are passed from SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud to Vertex as part of tax calculation or jurisdiction determination?

  • Is street-level address (e.g., street/house number) included in the payload, or is the integration primarily based on higher-level location data (e.g., ZIP/postal code, region)?

  • Do customers have visibility into or control over the payload being sent to Vertex in the standard Public Cloud integration?

  • For U.S. tax scenarios where jurisdiction accuracy can depend on street-level detail, how is this handled in the standard integration approach?

We are trying to determine whether the standard SAP-driven integration is sufficient for these requirements, or if tax determination is typically handled upstream (e.g., via middleware) to allow for more control and accuracy.

Any guidance or insights from those who have implemented Vertex with SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: SAP Public cloud analytics app and report
entry.content:
Dear @arturo_suarez,

List of the main standard apps and analytics related capabilities relevant for Manufacturing and Advanced Variant Configuration in Public Cloud

Manufacturing:

  • Manage Work Center Capacity (F3289)
  • Capacity Scheduling Table (F3770)
  • Analyze Production Costs Event Based (F4059)
  • Order Cost Details Event Based (F4254)
  • Event Based Solution Monitor Product Costing (F5133)
  • Manage Event Based Posting Errors Product Costing (F5132)
  • Postprocess Event Based Postings Overhead Calculation (F5763)
  • Inspection Lot Analytics (F3239)
  • Manage Defects (F2649)
  • Manage Inspection Lots (F2343)
  • Record Results for Inspection Points (F2689)
  • Production Cost Overview Event Based (F6248)
  • Manage Process Orders (F4587)
  • Manage Process Order Operations (F5323)

Advanced Variant Configuration:

  • VC Modeling Environment (PMEVC)
  • Simulate Configuration Models (F2570)
  • Variant Configuration Overview (F2940)
  • Order Engineering Worklist (F4770)
  • Variant Matrix Reuse Library Master Data (F3042)
  • Variant configuration Overview(F2940)
  • Custom CDS Views Version 2 (F1866A)
  • Custom Analytical Queries and Services (F1572A)
  • Query Browser (F1068)
  • View Browser (F2170)
  • Manage KPIs and Reports (F2814)
  • Analytical Queries (F1572)

More details below:

https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/homePage

Best Regards,

Dawid
